description:
AO already has a compiled state machine (10 states, 15 events, 34 transitions in state_machines/schema.rs) but it acts as a bookkeeping layer, not a routing layer. Phase advancement is positional (current_phase_index + 1), not driven by the state machine transitions. The Rework decision has a target_phase field but the executor ignores it — it just re-runs the current phase. Additionally, pipelines are JSON-only with no user-authored YAML surface.

## Current Architecture (what exists)
- WorkflowMachineState: Idle, EvaluateTransition, RunPhase, EvaluateGates, ApplyTransition, Paused, Completed, MergeConflict, Failed, Cancelled
- WorkflowDecisionAction: Advance, Skip, Rework, Repeat, Fail (with target_phase: Option<String>)
- WorkflowPhaseExecution.attempt: u32 (per-phase retry counter)
- MAX_PHASE_REWORKS=3 in lifecycle_executor.rs
- Phase capabilities (PhaseCapabilities from TASK-147)
- Checkpoint system (save/restore at phase boundaries)
- Research phase dynamic injection

## Gap Analysis
- Phase routing is index-based, not ID-based — Rework can't jump to a specific phase
- No skip conditions — phases always execute unless manually removed
- No conditional transitions (on_verdict routing) — code-review can't route to different phases based on verdict
- No escalation state — workflow goes straight to Failed when rework budget exceeds, no HumanEscalated
- No per-phase retry config — global MAX_PHASE_REWORKS applies to all phases equally
- No cross-phase data passing — each phase starts fresh with only task title/description
- No YAML surface — pipelines defined only in .ao/state/ JSON managed via CLI
- No command-mode phases — every phase requires an AI agent
- No parallel phases — strictly sequential execution

## Scope

### Phase 1: State Machine as Routing Layer
1. ID-based phase transitions: Replace current_phase_index with ID-based lookup. When Rework specifies target_phase, executor jumps to that phase.
2. on_verdict routing: Per-phase config declaring verdict-to-target routing (e.g., code-review rework -> implementation, advance -> testing).
3. skip_if conditions: Per-phase guards evaluated against task context (task_type, impact_area, capabilities).
4. HumanEscalated state: New state distinct from Paused/Failed. Entered on rework budget exceeded. Awaitable via ao workflow resume with feedback.
5. Per-phase retry config: max_attempts and retry_backoff configurable per phase.

### Phase 2: YAML Surface and Composability
6. YAML workflow definitions: ao.workflows.yaml committed to repo, compiles to internal JSON.
7. Cross-phase data passing: Persist phase output JSON, inject into next phase prompt via template variables.
8. Command-mode phases: Shell command phases (command: cargo test) with exit code + stdout as output.
9. Sub-pipeline composition: Reference other pipelines as nested steps.

### Phase 3: Advanced Patterns
10. Parallel phase execution: depends_on for DAG-style concurrency.
11. Trigger declarations: on: push, on: pr_opened, on: schedule for event-driven dispatch.
12. Loop constructs: Bounded iteration with exit conditions.

## References
- State machine: crates/orchestrator-core/src/state_machines/schema.rs
- FSM engine: crates/orchestrator-core/src/state_machines/engine.rs
- Phase advancement: crates/orchestrator-core/src/workflow/lifecycle_executor.rs
- Workflow config: crates/orchestrator-core/src/workflow_config.rs
- Phase executor: crates/orchestrator-cli/src/services/runtime/workflow_executor/phase_executor.rs
title: State Machine-Driven Workflow Engine with Composable YAML Pipelines
